data.title: [end-session]: merge-to-main.sh not found -> manual merge performed without DSO workflow
data.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When /dso:end-session runs merge-to-main, it should invoke merge-to-main.sh via the DSO shim (.claude/scripts/dso merge-to-main.sh) and use the full DSO merge workflow (sync → merge → version_bump → validate → push → archive → ci_trigger).

#### Actual Behavior

Error encountered: 'No merge-to-main.sh found. Performing merge manually.' The end-session skill falls back to a manual merge instead of failing loudly or guiding the user to install the shim correctly.

### 3. Likely Root Causes

1. The dso shim does not have merge-to-main.sh in its dispatch table
2. The CLAUDE.md or end-session SKILL.md uses a direct path instead of the shim
3. merge-to-main.sh is not present in the plugin cache used by the host project

### 4. Fix Direction

The end-session skill should:
1. Use .claude/scripts/dso merge-to-main.sh (via shim) exclusively
2. If the shim dispatch fails, emit a clear error with fix instructions (not silently fall back to manual merge)
3. Never perform manual git merge as a fallback — the DSO merge workflow exists to ensure proper state management
